The Practice

The art of the long hold

Yin is the quiet counterpart to our faster practices — long, supported holds that work past the muscle into the fascia, joints and connective tissue. It is where the body softens and the mind learns to stay.

This is not only a training for those who want to teach. It is four full days for anyone in need of deep restoration, or with a real passion for Yin and Traditional Chinese Medicine — and, should you wish, the foundation to teach. You leave able to hold a room in stillness.

Your Training

What's included

  • 50 hours of in-studio training & practice
  • Yoga Alliance accredited continuing education (50 CE)
  • Your own Ritual Yin Teacher Training manual to keep
  • The theory of Yin & Yang and Traditional Chinese Medicine
  • Functional anatomy of fascia & connective tissue
  • The meridian and organ-channel system
  • A full library of Yin poses & variations
  • Sequencing, theming & the art of cueing stillness
  • Use of props, bolsters & supported shapes
  • Practicum teaching with live feedback
  • Certificate of completion on graduation
  • Recommended reading: Yin Yoga by Bernie Clark
  • Tea, and a warm community for life
Ritual School

The curriculum

Six modules, fifty hours

Built so theory, anatomy and teaching practice deepen together — each day moves you closer to the seat of the teacher.

Module One

Foundations of Yin

History and philosophy, Yin & Yang theory, the principles of the long hold, and how Yin differs from restorative and Yang practice.

Module Two

Anatomy & Fascia

Functional anatomy, the connective tissue web, skeletal variation and "tension vs. compression" — why every body finds a pose differently.

Module Three

Meridians & Energetics

Traditional Chinese Medicine, the organ-meridian system, the five elements and how the channels map onto Yin postures.

Module Four

The Pose Library

A deep study of the core Yin shapes, their targets, variations, props and contraindications — built into your teaching toolkit.

Module Five

Sequencing & Theming

Building meridian-based and seasonal sequences, language and cueing for stillness, music, silence, and holding space.

Module Six

The Seat of the Teacher

Practicum teaching, adjustments and the art of presence, ethics, scope of practice, and stepping into your first class.

How it runs

The schedule

Weekend One

Saturday 6 June · 8:30am–5:30pm & Sunday 7 June · 9:00am–6:15pm. Foundations of Yin, anatomy and the fascial body.

Weekend Two

Saturday 13 June · 8:30am–5:30pm & Sunday 14 June · 9:00am–6:15pm. Meridians, sequencing and the seat of the teacher.

Mornings

Theory, philosophy and anatomy, followed by a guided Yin practice to feel each concept in the body.

Afternoons

Pose breakdown, sequencing labs and practicum teaching with feedback from Alex.

Meet Your Teacher

Alex Vannucci

Yin Teacher · Acupuncturist · TCM

Alex has a profound interest in the biomechanics of the body and the dynamic flow of energy within. During her studies in Nutritional Therapy she felt a strong calling to deepen her practice in India, where she studied yoga under the esteemed Yogarishi Vishvaketu at Anand Prakash Ashram in Rishikesh — a transformative experience that inspired her to integrate holistic yoga and meditation into her life and pursue it as a career.

Her unquenchable curiosity about the human body led her to further studies across many yoga styles, as well as Mat and Reformer Pilates with Polestar Pilates International, and Zen meditation under Mark Togni on the Gold Coast. Alex is also a qualified acupuncturist, bringing a rich understanding of Traditional Chinese Medicine and energetic systems into her teaching — allowing students to explore Yin not only through a biomechanical lens, but through the deeper energetic theories that underpin the practice.

As Alex continues to expand her knowledge, her passion for guiding others to trust their intuition and evolve into their best selves grows daily. She plays a key role in facilitating Ritual's Yoga Teacher Training courses, sharing her deep knowledge of biomechanics, yoga, meditation and Traditional Chinese Medicine to inspire the next generation of instructors.

Good to know

Questions

Do I have to want to teach?

Not at all. Around half our trainees come to deepen their own practice. You'll be welcomed either way.

What experience do I need?

A love of slow, mindful and gentle yoga. No prior teacher training required.

Is it accredited?

Yes — the 50 hours count as Yoga Alliance continuing education for registered teachers, and you receive a Ritual certificate of completion.

Can I pay in instalments?

Yes. A deposit holds your place and the balance can be split into a payment plan — just ask us.
